query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "qwen-agentworld: language world models for general agents" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (f) as it investigates the fundamental mechanism of "compositionality" in language emergence among artificial agents, which is a core linguistic property relevant to the user's query about extending language world models. Although it uses different terminology ("emergent communication" vs. "linguistics" in world models) and a specific RL setup, it provides canonical prior work on the linguistic structure of agent languages that a literature review on this to
